title: Demonstration gallery
text: Summary: City Sydney Parramatta assessed highest number applications Q1 2025, together accounting third total. Source: NSW Planning Portal. Last updated: March 2025.

title: Demonstration gallery
text: Summary: Digital contact grew steadily across four quarters, phone -person volumes declined. Q4, digital accounted two-thirds requests. Source: Service NSW. Last updated: June 2025.

title: Demonstration gallery
text: Summary: Incidents highest summer months, peaking December January. July recorded lowest monthly total year. Source: Transport NSW. Last updated: December 2024.

title: Demonstration gallery
text: Summary: App transactions grew fastest year, nearly doubling. Web remained largest channel. Assisted digital declined steadily self-service adoption increased. Source: Service NSW. Last updated: June 2025.

title: Demonstration gallery
text: Summary: Grant disbursements accelerated October, final quarter accounting half full-year total. Source: NSW Treasury. Last updated: December 2024.

title: Demonstration gallery
text: Summary: total number open cases grew steadily across priority levels. Critical cases grew fastest proportionally, nearly doubling January June. Source: Service NSW. Last updated: June 2025.

title: Demonstration gallery
text: Summary: Applications higher complexity scores consistently took longer process. straightforward applications (score 30) resolved within 5 working days. Complex applications (score 70) averaged 18 days, significant variation. Source: NSW Planning Portal. Last updated: March 2025.

title: cookbook
text: standard ggplot output:  Style waratah:  Change different NSW palette:  markdown handling done ggtext. See vignette("theme_elements", package = "ggtext") details.
code: library(waratah) library(palmerpenguins) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  long_text <- paste(   c(     "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do",     "eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim",     "ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut",     "aliquip ex ea commodo consequat."   ),   collapse = " " )  p1 <-   ggplot(penguins) +   geom_point(aes(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = flipper_length_mm,     colour = species,     size = body_mass_g   )) +   labs(     title = "Let's try some *italics* in the title",     subtitle = long_text,     dictionary = c(       bill_length_mm = "Bill length (mm)",       flipper_length_mm = "Flipper length (mm)",       species = "Species",       body_mass_g = "Body mass (g)"     ),     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   )  p1 #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). p1 +   theme_waratah() #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). p1 +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = pal_waratah("qual", var = "aboriginal")) +   theme_waratah() #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: cookbook
text: standard ggplot output:  Styling using waratah package - want paired colour palette:  manual control palette possible, example specifying rows columns colour grid use:
code: p2 <-   filter_out(penguins, is.na(sex)) |>   ggplot() +   geom_point(     aes(       x = bill_length_mm,       y = bill_depth_mm,       fill = interaction(sex, species),     ),     shape = 21,     size = 3,     colour = "black",     alpha = 0.8   ) +   labs(     title = "Penguin bill dimensions",     subtitle = "Separated by *species* and *sex*",     dictionary = c(       bill_length_mm = "Bill length (mm)",       bill_depth_mm = "Bill depth (mm)"     ),     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   ) +   guides(     fill = legendry::guide_legend_group(       title = NULL,       ncol = 1,       direction = "horizontal",       override.aes = list(size = 3)     )   )  p2 + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired") p2 +   discrete_scale("fill", palette = pal_waratah("pairs")) +   theme_waratah() p2 +   scale_fill_discrete(     palette = pal_nsw(       t = 2:3,       h = c("purple", "blue", "red"),       var = "aboriginal"     )   ) +   theme_waratah(base_size = 14)

title: cookbook
text: standard ggplot output  Styling using waratah package - specifying pallete
code: p3 <-   filter_out(penguins, is.na(sex)) |>   ggplot(aes(x = species, fill = island)) +   geom_bar() +   labs(     dictionary = c(species = "Species", island = "Island"),     title = "Perfectly proportional penguins",     subtitle = "Where do they all live?",     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   ) +   facet_wrap(vars(sex))  p3 p3 +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = pal_nsw(hue = "purples")) +   theme_waratah(base_size = 14) +   guides(x = guide_axis(angle = 70)) +   theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())

title: cookbook
text: standard ggplot output:  Styling using waratah package options:
code: p4 <-   ggplot(penguins) +   geom_bar(aes(1, fill = species)) +   geom_text(     aes(       1,       y = after_stat(count),       label = stage(         species,         after_stat = sprintf("%s\n%.0f%%", label, 100 * count / sum(count))       ),       colour = stage(         species,         after_scale = col_contrasting(colour)       )     ),     stat = "count",     position = position_stack(vjust = .5),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   coord_radial(     theta = "y",     inner.radius = .5,     expand = FALSE,     rotate.angle = TRUE   ) +   labs(     title = "Does anyone know if penguins like doughnuts?",     subtitle = "Not sure, but we know there are three species in the dataset",     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   )  p4 p4 +   scale_fill_discrete(     aesthetics = c("fill", "colour"),     palette = pal_waratah("qual"),     guide = "none"   ) +   theme_waratah(     void = TRUE,     base_size = 14   )

title: cookbook
text: Likert-scale survey responses can handled dedicated tools like ggstats::gglikert(). self-contained ggplot stat achieves limited version result purposes demonstrating styling. standard ggplot output:  Styling using waratah package. Note diverging palette continuous, need pal_stretch() discretise . specify "red" starting hue. Without cvd = TRUE second colour ends green.
code: StatLikert <- ggproto(   "StatLikert",   StatIdentity,   compute_layer = function(self, data, params, layout) {     mutate(       data,       offset = sum(x[1:(n() %/% 2)]),       offset = if_else(         rep(n() %% 2 > 0, n()),         offset + x[n() %/% 2 + 1] / 2,         offset       ),       left = cumsum(lag(x, default = 0)) - offset,       right = cumsum(x) - offset,       x = (left + right) / 2,       width = right - left,       .by = y     )   } ) sentiment <- c(   "Strongly Disagree",   "Disagree",   "Neutral",   "Agree",   "Strongly Agree" )  survey_data <- tibble(   answer = factor(rep(sentiment, 2), levels = sentiment, ordered = TRUE),   percent = c(8, 12, 30, 40, 10, 6, 18, 24, 34, 18),   group = sort(rep(c("Male", "Female"), 5)) )  p5 <-   ggplot(     survey_data,     aes(x = percent, y = group, fill = answer)   ) +   geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 0, colour = from_theme(accent))) +   # note: likert is not a standard stat - it's defined above   geom_tile(stat = "likert", height = 0.8) +   labs(     title = "Let's find out!",     subtitle = "How much do they agree with the statement \"Doughnuts are delicious\"?",     caption = "Totally made up data!",     x = NULL,     y = NULL,     fill = "Answer"   ) +   theme(     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),     legend.position = "bottom"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(abs(x), "%"))  p5 + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "PiYG") p5 +   discrete_scale(     "fill",     palette = pal_waratah("div", "red", cvd = TRUE) |> pal_stretch(),     breaks = sentiment   ) +   theme_waratah(base_size = 12)

title: waratah
text: NSW Government typeface Public Sans. Recommendations: Install font computer. package attempt register embedded copy font can used even without installing font first. possible use graphics device supports modern features ragg::agg_png(). devices used default RStudio {ragg} installed. using HTML output (interactive plots tables), ensure document loads Public Sans Google Fonts (see ). Either add <head>: add CSS:
code: <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Public+Sans:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400&display=swap" rel="stylesheet"> @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Public+Sans:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400&display=swap');

title: waratah
text: theme_waratah() sets default fonts, styles, colour palettes plots. can set theme globally ggplot2::set_theme() add individual plot. using global options like options(waratah.colour_theme = "aboriginal"), make sure setting theme. See vignette("cookbook") examples using theme.

title: waratah
text: NSW palettes designed around two grids consisting colour columns tonal rows. Guidelines charts suggest techniques ensuring good contrast charts accessible. Individual colours available name, e.g. nsw_colours$blue_01.

title: waratah
text: access colours using grid system, use pal_nsw(). can visualise colours utility scales package:  gave us first two tones blue followed first two tones red. Importantly, pal_nsw() always provides discrete colour palette. use ggplot discrete colour scale, can use: scale_fill_discrete() set fill scale. case need continuous scale, ggplot automatically interpolate colours:
code: pal_nsw(hue = c("blue", "red"), tone = 1:2) |> scales::show_col() scale_colour_discrete(palette = pal_nsw(hue = c("blue", "red"), tone = 1:2)) scale_colour_continuous(palette = pal_nsw(hue = "blue"))

title: waratah
text: Instead working directly grid, can instead use pal_waratah(). Specify pattern data get reasonable discrete continous scale. Note makes harder follow chat recommendations mentioned , can convenient scientific data visualisation. qualitative data, mix tonal rows 1 2 chosen avoid overly-similar colours:  also palettes sequential diverging continuous data allow choice base hue:  pal_waratah() considers colour similarity, can request also take account colour vision disorders:  make choice globally, set:
code: pal_waratah("qual") |> scales::show_col() pal_waratah("div", hue = "green") |> scales::show_col(labels = FALSE) pal_waratah("div", hue = "green", cvd = TRUE) |> scales::show_col(labels = FALSE) options(waratah.cvd = TRUE)

title: waratah
text: Colour palettes waratah allow variant specified, including default variant = "base" Aboriginal palette variant = "aboriginal". See pal_nsw() details.

title: waratah
text: logo included PNG format:
code: library(magick) #> Linking to ImageMagick 6.9.12.98 #> Enabled features: fontconfig, freetype, fftw, heic, lcms, pango, raw, webp, x11 #> Disabled features: cairo, ghostscript, rsvg #> Using 4 threads image_path <- system.file(   "images",   "nsw-gov-logo-primary.png",   package = "waratah" ) image_read(image_path)

what: Easy data viz - NSW Government colour & typography design
title: Easy data viz - NSW Government colour & typography design
text: goal waratah provide R tool kit styles, patterns standards following Digital NSW, NSW Design System

title: Easy data viz - NSW Government colour & typography design
text: can install waratah like :
code: # CRAN release (not yet!) # install.packages('waratah')  # development version install.packages('pak') pak::pak('digitalnsw/nsw-r-visualisations')

title: Easy data viz - NSW Government colour & typography design
text: many instances may sufficient set global theme:  control available palette functions pal_waratah(). See vignette("waratah") usage guidelines.
code: library(ggplot2) library(waratah)  set_theme(theme_waratah())  ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = class)) +   geom_point()

what: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
title: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
text: NSW design system colours work grids colour columns tonal rows. col_nsw() allows accessing colour grid like matrix. ggplot colour palettes, normally want pal_nsw() instead. define_colour_theme() defines new colour theme can used waratah function accepts variant parameter.

title: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
code: nsw_colours  define_colour_theme(name, parent, colours)  col_nsw(   hue,   tone,   variant = getOption("waratah.colour_theme", default = "base"),   byrow = FALSE )

title: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
text: name name new colour theme. parent name parent grid theme, usually "base" "aboriginal". colours character vector colour column names. hue name index hue - see . tone name index tone - see . variant name palette variant. Available options : base, aboriginal, corporate, treasury. byrow vary tone faster hue TRUE.

title: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
text: col_nsw() named vector colours, define_colour_theme() nothing: called side effects.

title: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
text: hue tone work way matrix indexing [ can used return single multiple entries grid. can character vectors, integer vectors, logicals. Anchor colours used create NSW colour palettes can also used stand-alone (e.g. nsw_colours$blue_01 "#002664").

title: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
text: "base" variant supports: hue: greys, greens, teals, blues, purples, fucshias, reds, oranges, yellows, browns tone: dark, normal, light, pale "aboriginal" variant supports: hue: reds, oranges, browns, yellows, greens, blues, purples, greys tone: dark, normal, light, pale Colour themes support subsets hues one main grids specific order. themes built : "treasury": teals, greys, oranges, greens "corporate": blues, reds, greys default variant can specified globally options(waratah.colour_theme). Unambiguous shortened forms accepted, e.g. pal_nsw(h = "red", v = "").

title: Access NSW palette grids by rows and/or columns — nsw_colours
code: col_nsw(h = "blue", v = "aboriginal") #>   billabong_blue   saltwater_blue light_water_blue     coastal_blue  #>        "#00405e"        "#0d6791"        "#84c5d1"        "#c1e2e8"  col_nsw(h = c("teal", "orange"), t = 1:2) #>   teal_01   teal_02 orange_01 orange_02  #> "#0b3f47" "#2e808e" "#941b00" "#f3631b"  col_nsw(h = c("teal", "orange"), t = 1:2, byrow = TRUE) #>   teal_01 orange_01   teal_02 orange_02  #> "#0b3f47" "#941b00" "#2e808e" "#f3631b"

title: Construct palette variants — pal_interleave
text: Depending structure data may wish combine palettes according pattern. helpers may come handy. pal_c() concatenates multiple discrtee palettes. pal_interleave() interleaves colours multiple palettes size. helps data grouped need flexibility colour grid. pal_stretch() interpolates palette new discrete palette. useful stretching 4 tones 5 6, expense straying NSW grid. col_contrasting() chooses colours based given background colours. helps drawing text top mapped (.e. variable) fill aesthetic

title: Construct palette variants — pal_interleave
code: pal_interleave(...)  pal_c(...)  pal_stretch(pal)  col_contrasting(colour, light = "white", dark = "black")

title: Construct palette variants — pal_interleave
text: ... two vectors colours. pal palette object. colour vector colours. light, dark colours output colour dark light respectively.

title: Construct palette variants — pal_interleave
text: col_contrasting() vector colours length colour, pal_*() palette object.

what: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
title: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
text: Palettes created using NSW Design System. use Aboriginal colour grid, specify variant = "aboriginal".

title: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
code: pal_nsw(   palette = waiver(),   hue = NA,   tone = NA,   variant = getOption("waratah.colour_theme", default = "base"),   direction = 1 )  pal_nsw_manual(colours)

title: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
text: palette name predefined palette: default, brand_default, core. hue name index hue - see . Ignored palette specified. tone name index tone - see . Ignored palette specified. variant name palette variant. Available options : base, aboriginal, corporate, treasury. Ignored unless hue tone specified. direction set -1 reverse order colours palette, 1 original order. colours vector colour names corresponding nsw_colours.

title: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
text: palette object (see palette constructors)

title: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
text: use palettes based NSW Design System colour grids, either specify hue allow tone vary, specify tone allow hue vary. recommendation use first two tonal rows going one colour time set colours; can achieved specifying tone = 1:2. several named palettes can specified palette. create custom combinations named colours design system, use pal_nsw_manual().

title: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
text: "base" variant supports: hue: greys, greens, teals, blues, purples, fucshias, reds, oranges, yellows, browns tone: dark, normal, light, pale "aboriginal" variant supports: hue: reds, oranges, browns, yellows, greens, blues, purples, greys tone: dark, normal, light, pale Colour themes support subsets hues one main grids specific order. themes built : "treasury": teals, greys, oranges, greens "corporate": blues, reds, greys default variant can specified globally options(waratah.colour_theme). Unambiguous shortened forms accepted, e.g. pal_nsw(h = "red", v = "").

title: NSW Design System colour palettes — pal_nsw
code: library(scales)  pal_nsw() |> show_col()  pal_nsw(hue = "blues") |> show_col()  pal_nsw(tone = 1:2, variant = "corporate") |> show_col()  pal_nsw(tone = "light") |> show_col()  pal_nsw(tone = "normal", variant = "aboriginal") |> show_col()  pal_nsw_manual(c("blue_02", "red_01", "green_03")) |> show_col()   # you can interpolate colours by converting to a continuous scale pal_nsw(hue = "blues") |> as_continuous_pal() |> show_col(labels = FALSE)

title: Flexible colour palettes — pal_waratah
text: Unlike pal_nsw(), pal_waratah() based strictly NSW colour palette grids. tries choose colours perceptually distinct, optionally taking account colour-blindness .

title: Flexible colour palettes — pal_waratah
code: pal_waratah(   type = c("qual", "seq", "div", "pairs", "triples"),   hue = 1,   cvd = getOption("waratah.cvd", default = FALSE),   variant = getOption("waratah.colour_theme", default = "base"),   direction = 1 )

title: Flexible colour palettes — pal_waratah
text: type type palette generated: "qual" discrete qualitative palette "seq" continuous sequential palette: gradient spanning extreme tones hue "div" continuous diverging palette: gradient spanning extreme tones hue second distinct colour. "pairs", "triples" like `"qual"“ sets 2 2 tones hue. hue main hue, either name index. See col_nsw() supported values. used type "seq" "div". cvd TRUE account colour vision disorders. Requires colorBlindness package. variant name palette variant. Available options : base, aboriginal, corporate, treasury. Ignored unless hue tone specified. direction set -1 reverse order colours palette, 1 original order.

title: Flexible colour palettes — pal_waratah
text: palette object (see palette constructors)

title: Flexible colour palettes — pal_waratah
code: library(scales)  pal_waratah("qual") |> show_col()  pal_waratah("pairs") |> show_col()  pal_waratah("seq", hue = "red") |> show_col(labels = FALSE)  pal_waratah("div", hue = "yellow", variant = "aboriginal") |>   show_col(labels = FALSE)

what: Theme for reactable interactive tables — reactable_theme
title: Theme for reactable interactive tables — reactable_theme
text: using reactable, helper constructs theme.

title: Theme for reactable interactive tables — reactable_theme
code: reactable_theme(   colour = "blue_01",   text_colour = colour,   borderColor = colour,   backgroundColor = "white",   base_family = "Public Sans",   title_family = "Public Sans",   base_text_size = 10,   stripedColor = NA,   ... )

title: Theme for reactable interactive tables — reactable_theme
text: colour starting colour used set common default colour parameters. text_colour colour text. borderColor colour horizontal lines rows. backgroundColor background colour. base_family font family normal text. title_family font family title text. base_text_size base text size pt. header size relative base text size. stripedColor fill colour alternate rows. NA, set blend borderColor backgroundColor. ... parameters pass reactable::reactableTheme(). examples can done reactable, see https://glin.github.io/reactable/articles/cookbook/cookbook.html.

title: Theme for reactable interactive tables — reactable_theme
text: reactable theme object

title: Theme for reactable interactive tables — reactable_theme
text: Colours can specified named NSW colours nsw_colours. normal HTML elements, must make sure final document loads necessary fonts. See vignette("waratah") instructions.

title: Theme for reactable interactive tables — reactable_theme
code: library(reactable)  # The default theme head(palmerpenguins::penguins, 10) |>   reactable(theme = reactableTheme())  {"x":{"tag":{"name":"Reactable","attribs":{"data":{"species":["Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie"],"island":["Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen"],"bill_length_mm":[39.1,39.5,40.3,"NA",36.7,39.3,38.9,39.2,34.1,42],"bill_depth_mm":[18.7,17.4,18,"NA",19.3,20.6,17.8,19.6,18.1,20.2],"flipper_length_mm":[181,186,195,"NA",193,190,181,195,193,190],"body_mass_g":[3750,3800,3250,"NA",3450,3650,3625,4675,3475,4250],"sex":["male","female","female",null,"female","male","female","male",null,null],"year":[2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007]},"columns":[{"id":"species","name":"species","type":"factor"},{"id":"island","name":"island","type":"factor"},{"id":"bill_length_mm","name":"bill_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"bill_depth_mm","name":"bill_depth_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"flipper_length_mm","name":"flipper_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"body_mass_g","name":"body_mass_g","type":"numeric"},{"id":"sex","name":"sex","type":"factor"},{"id":"year","name":"year","type":"numeric"}],"dataKey":"d09fc92c32ed54493f36a19991012b2e"},"children":[]},"class":"reactR_markup"},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]} # Adding waratah style head(palmerpenguins::penguins, 10) |>   reactable(theme = reactable_theme())  {"x":{"tag":{"name":"Reactable","attribs":{"data":{"species":["Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie"],"island":["Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen"],"bill_length_mm":[39.1,39.5,40.3,"NA",36.7,39.3,38.9,39.2,34.1,42],"bill_depth_mm":[18.7,17.4,18,"NA",19.3,20.6,17.8,19.6,18.1,20.2],"flipper_length_mm":[181,186,195,"NA",193,190,181,195,193,190],"body_mass_g":[3750,3800,3250,"NA",3450,3650,3625,4675,3475,4250],"sex":["male","female","female",null,"female","male","female","male",null,null],"year":[2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007]},"columns":[{"id":"species","name":"species","type":"factor"},{"id":"island","name":"island","type":"factor"},{"id":"bill_length_mm","name":"bill_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"bill_depth_mm","name":"bill_depth_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"flipper_length_mm","name":"flipper_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"body_mass_g","name":"body_mass_g","type":"numeric"},{"id":"sex","name":"sex","type":"factor"},{"id":"year","name":"year","type":"numeric"}],"theme":{"backgroundColor":"white","borderColor":"#002664","stripedColor":"#B2BED0FF","style":{"color":"#002664","fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"10pt"},"tableStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"10pt"},"headerStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"12pt"},"cellStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"10pt"}},"dataKey":"d09fc92c32ed54493f36a19991012b2e"},"children":[]},"class":"reactR_markup"},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]} # More customised styling head(palmerpenguins::penguins, 10) |>   reactable(     theme = reactable_theme(       colour = "blue_01",       base_family = "Arial",       text_colour = "black",       backgroundColor = NULL     ),     striped = TRUE   )  {"x":{"tag":{"name":"Reactable","attribs":{"data":{"species":["Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie"],"island":["Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen"],"bill_length_mm":[39.1,39.5,40.3,"NA",36.7,39.3,38.9,39.2,34.1,42],"bill_depth_mm":[18.7,17.4,18,"NA",19.3,20.6,17.8,19.6,18.1,20.2],"flipper_length_mm":[181,186,195,"NA",193,190,181,195,193,190],"body_mass_g":[3750,3800,3250,"NA",3450,3650,3625,4675,3475,4250],"sex":["male","female","female",null,"female","male","female","male",null,null],"year":[2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007]},"columns":[{"id":"species","name":"species","type":"factor"},{"id":"island","name":"island","type":"factor"},{"id":"bill_length_mm","name":"bill_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"bill_depth_mm","name":"bill_depth_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"flipper_length_mm","name":"flipper_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"body_mass_g","name":"body_mass_g","type":"numeric"},{"id":"sex","name":"sex","type":"factor"},{"id":"year","name":"year","type":"numeric"}],"striped":true,"theme":{"borderColor":"#002664","stripedColor":[],"style":{"color":"black","fontFamily":"Arial","fontSize":"10pt"},"tableStyle":{"fontFamily":"Arial","fontSize":"10pt"},"headerStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"12pt"},"cellStyle":{"fontFamily":"Arial","fontSize":"10pt"}},"dataKey":"d09fc92c32ed54493f36a19991012b2e"},"children":[]},"class":"reactR_markup"},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]}

what: Theme plots using NSW colours and fonts — theme_waratah
title: Theme plots using NSW colours and fonts — theme_waratah
text: 'ggplot2' theme compatible NSW design system. sets default colour scales, fonts, styles.

title: Theme plots using NSW colours and fonts — theme_waratah
code: theme_waratah(   base_size = 11,   base_family = "Public Sans",   header_family = "Public Sans",   base_line_size = base_size/22,   base_rect_size = base_size/22,   ink = "black",   paper = "white",   geom_ink = "blue_01",   accent = "blue_02",   variant = getOption("waratah.colour_theme", default = "base"),   show_grid_lines = TRUE,   void = FALSE )

title: Theme plots using NSW colours and fonts — theme_waratah
text: base_size base font size, given pts. base_family base font family header_family font family titles headers. default, NULL, uses theme inheritance set font. setting affects axis titles, legend titles, plot title tag text. base_line_size base size line elements base_rect_size base size rect elements ink, paper, accent colour foreground, background, accented elements respectively. geom_ink default ink colour used geoms points, lines fills. variant name palette variant. Available options : base, aboriginal, corporate, treasury. show_grid_lines whether show grid lines. FALSE, grid lines removed axis text retained. Ignored void TRUE. void whether hide grid lines axes. TRUE, grid lines axes removed. useful creating pie/donut charts.

title: Theme plots using NSW colours and fonts — theme_waratah
text: ggplot theme specification add plot

title: Theme plots using NSW colours and fonts — theme_waratah
code: library(ggplot2) set_theme(theme_waratah())  ggplot(palmerpenguins::penguins) +   geom_point(aes(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = flipper_length_mm,     colour = species,     size = body_mass_g   )) +   labs(     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}",     dictionary = c(       bill_length_mm = "Bill length (mm)",       flipper_length_mm = "Flipper length (mm)",       species = "Species",       body_mass_g = "Body mass (g)"     )   ) #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: Style interactive plot tooltips — tooltip_css
text: CSS code required add waratah-styled tooltips interactive graphs created using ggiraph.

title: Style interactive plot tooltips — tooltip_css
code: tooltip_css(   background_colour = "grey_01",   text_colour = "off_white",   font_family = "\"Public Sans\", Arial, sans",   font_size = "11pt" )

title: Style interactive plot tooltips — tooltip_css
text: background_colour tooltip background colour text_colour tooltip text colour font_family font family text tooltips font_size font size text tooltips

title: Style interactive plot tooltips — tooltip_css
text: character vector containing CSS rules

title: Style interactive plot tooltips — tooltip_css
text: normal HTML elements, must make sure final document loads necessary fonts. See vignette("waratah") instructions.

title: Style interactive plot tooltips — tooltip_css
code: tooltip_css()
